title: ELC Costs for Fluid Dynamics Equations
note_ELC: ELC(R) = exp-log closure over the reals. sin/cos have infinitely many zeros so are OUTSIDE real ELC (T01).

equations.name: Reynolds number
equations.domain_notes: All quantities strictly positive in physical flow; mul cost 2n applies.

equations.name: Bernoulli equation

term1_P.desc: Static pressure — no arithmetic ops (raw variable)

equations.domain_notes: rho, v, g, h all positive in typical flow; P is positive gauge pressure.

equations.name: Navier-Stokes (incompressible 2D, one velocity component u)
equations.note: Spatial derivatives treated as known inputs (evaluated elsewhere). Cost is purely algebraic combination.
equations.domain_notes: rho, nu strictly positive. du/dt RHS is a real-valued combination; no trig involved in this algebraic form.

equations.name: Drag equation
equations.domain_notes: All quantities positive (speed magnitude, density, area, drag coefficient).

equations.name: Poiseuille flow (volumetric flow rate)
equations.formula: Q = pi * r^4 * DeltaP / (8 * mu * L)
equations.note_pow4: pow(r,4) taken as a single pow at 3n cost (SuperBEST v5 pow is constant-cost per call, not degree-dependent). Alternative: pow(r,2) then pow(r^2,2) costs 6n — direct pow cheaper.
equations.domain_notes: r, mu, L strictly positive; DeltaP positive (pressure drop driving flow).

equations.name: Stokes drag
equations.note: 6*pi is a known numeric constant, folded into first mul.
equations.domain_notes: Pure multiplication chain; all quantities positive (viscosity, radius, speed).

equations.name: Turbulent kinetic energy
equations.domain_notes: Fluctuation components can be negative — however squaring makes them positive before summation. No domain restriction violation.

equations.name: Kolmogorov length scale
equations.domain_notes: nu (kinematic viscosity) and epsilon (dissipation rate) strictly positive. (1/4) power is a valid positive-real pow.

equations.name: Strouhal number
equations.domain_notes: f (frequency), D (diameter), v (velocity) all positive. Dimensionless ratio. No trig.
equations.trig_note: Strouhal number itself is trig-free and cleanly inside ELC.

equations.name: Vortex shedding frequency via Strouhal
equations.formula_position: theta(t) = integral of omega(t) dt  [requires sin/cos for x(t), y(t)]

vortex_position.reason: Vortex position in 2D requires x(t) = r*cos(theta(t)), y(t) = r*sin(theta(t)). Both sin and cos are OUTSIDE real ELC because they have infinitely many zeros on R (T01 criterion). Any formula tracking the angular position or transverse oscillation of a shed vortex necessarily involves trig.

equations.trig_note: The scalar shedding frequency f is inside ELC; the full vortex trajectory is outside real ELC.

summary_table.name: Vortex shedding (frequency)
summary_table.caveat: vortex POSITION (x(t),y(t)) requires sin/cos → OUTSIDE ELC

geometric_wave_boundary.observation: Fluid dynamics splits cleanly along the geometric/viscous vs. rotational/wave boundary.
inside_ELC: Poiseuille flow (pipe flow, radial geometry via pow)
Turbulent kinetic energy (sum of squared fluctuations)
Kolmogorov scale (power-law of viscosity and dissipation)
outside_ELC: Vortex position x(t) = r*cos(omega*t), y(t) = r*sin(omega*t)
Rotating frame transformations (sin/cos of rotation angle)
Streamlines in potential flow with circulation: psi involves atan2
geometric_wave_boundary.principle: Equations involving only magnitudes, ratios, power-laws, and linear combinations of physical scalars are inside ELC(R). Equations tracking angular position, wave phase, or spatial oscillation require sin/cos and are outside real ELC. This boundary aligns with: geometric/viscous phenomena (ELC-amenable) vs. rotational/wave/acoustic phenomena (trig-essential).
